segments.text: Do not judge, or you too will be judged.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother's eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' when all the time there is a plank in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do not give dogs what is sacred; do not throw your pearls to pigs. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of you, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you, then, though you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you, for this sums up the Law and the Prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ferocious wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By their fruit you will recognize them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Thus, by their fruit you will recognize them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Not everyone who says to me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many will say to me on that day, 'Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name and in your name drive out demons and in your name perform many miracles?'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then I will tell them plainly, 'I never knew you. Away from me, you evildoers!'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock.
But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash.
When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching,
because he taught as one who had authority, and not as their teachers of the law.
